title: [Docker Compatibility] Endpoint /images/json will return empty body with status code 200 if `digests` is specified
body: ### Issue Description When using the compat endpoint to list images, with the `digests` query, podman will return an empty body, with a status code 200, leading to very weird behavior when you don't expect this. ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. In one terminal run `podman system service --time=0 tcp://localhost:8888` 2. In another terminal run `curl --location 'http://localhost:8888/images/json?digests=true'` 2. Assert empty body ### Describe the results you received Receiving an empty body with status code 200. ### Describe the results you expected Either an error, or the digests option to be ignored I know from the documentation[^1] that this is not supported, but the empty response is a bit brutal way of dealing with this as it broke our system [^1]: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=latest#tag/images-(compat)/operation/ImageList ### podman info output ```yaml $ podman info WARN[0000] The storage 'driver' option should be set in /etc/containers/storage.conf. A driver was picked automatically. host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.43.2 cgroupControllers: - cpu - io - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.2.1-2.fc44.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.2.1, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 89.12 systemPercent: 1.04 userPercent: 9.84 cpus: 16 databaseBackend: sqlite distribution: distribution: fedora variant: workstation version: "44" emulatedArchitectures: - linux/arm - linux/arm64 - linux/arm64be - linux/loong64 - linux/mips - linux/mips64 - linux/ppc - linux/ppc64 - linux/ppc64le - linux/riscv32 - linux/riscv64 - linux/s390x eventLogger: journald freeLocks: 1997 hostname: fedora.home idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 524288 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 524288 size: 65536 kernel: 7.1.3-201.fc44.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 14539620352 memTotal: 67104686080 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark defaultNetwork: podman dns: package: aardvark-dns-1.17.1-1.fc44.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.17.1 package: netavark-1.17.2-1.fc44.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.17.2 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.28-1.fc44.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.28 commit: 54f16ffbefcd022bf032af768b5c5ce075c18bfc rundir: /run/user/1000/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +WASM:wasmedge +JSON_C os: linux pasta: executable: /usr/bin/pasta package: passt-0^20260611.ga9c61ff-1.fc44.x86_64 version: | pasta 0^20260611.ga9c61ff-1.fc44.x86_64 Copyright Red Hat GNU General Public License, version 2 or later This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law. remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock rootlessNetworkCmd: pasta security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 8589201408 swapTotal: 8589930496 uptime: 5h 10m 59.00s (Approximately 0.21 days) variant: "" plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - docker.io store: configFile: /home/axel7083/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 10 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 10 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/axel7083/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 1022505254912 graphRootUsed: 725975891968 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: btrfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Supports shifting: "false" Supports volatile: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 1727 runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers transientStore: false volumePath: /home/axel7083/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 5.8.4 BuildOrigin: Fedora Project Built: 1782432000 BuiltTime: Fri Jun 26 02:00:00 2026 GitCommit: 5431df23c742e5edea35bef34eed696f4db0106b GoVersion: go1.26.4-X:nodwarf5 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 5.8.4 ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Rootless ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details Additional environment details ### Additional information Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
So looking a the code the it shows a info log on the server which of course is rather useless for any client and then just does the normal listing. So the empty result is just because of the system not having any image otherwise it would show the regular images.

What exactly does the digests option do? If it is something simple we can expose it seems best to just enable and support that option. If it is to complex I guess we should return a HTTP 400 error saying the digest option is unsupported.
Ah nevermind, I overlooked the early return. yeah returning without any body is really wrong.

> What exactly does the digests option do? If it is something simple we can expose it seems best to just enable and support that option. If it is to complex I guess we should return a HTTP 400 error saying the digest option is unsupported.

Reading the Docker Engine v1.55 we can read, _Show digest information as a RepoDigests field on each image._[^1]

[^1]: https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.55/#tag/Image/operation/ImageList

I feel like on the libpod endpoint, we are already returning the digests through the `RepoDigests`, so the easy fix might be to support this option 🤔
